title: Cynanchum thesioides
intro: Cynanchum thesioides, also known as Qingye, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine. The health benefits of Qingye include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties, which have been shown to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and inhibit the growth of cancer cells. The bioactive constituents of Qingye include diterpenoid lactones, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Qingye is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Cynanchum thesioides is classified in the following manner: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, the class Magnoliopsida, the order Gentianales, and the family Apocynaceae. The genus Cynanchum is a group of plants that are commonly known as swallowworts.
